chapter_title.ar: بَابُ النَّهْيِ عَنِ الْقَوْلِ بِغَيْرِ عِلْمٍ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 11 – The forbiddance from the speech without knowledge

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (138/ 1)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (138/ 1)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) مَا حَقُّ اللَّهِ عَلَى الْعِبَادِ قَالَ أَنْ يَقُولُوا مَا يَعْلَمُونَ وَ يَقِفُوا عِنْدَ مَا لَا يَعْلَمُونَ .

en.hubeali: Al Husayn Bin Muhammad, from Moalla Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Asbat, from Ja’far Bin Sama’at, from someone else, from Aban, from Zurara Bin Ayn who said,

‘I asked Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, ‘What is a Right of Allah<sup>azwj</sup> upon the servants?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘That he should be saying what he knows, and refraining during what he does not know’.
en.sarwar: 7. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad has narrated from Mu‘alla ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn Asbat from Ja’far ibn Sama‘a from several persons, from aban, from Zurara ibn A‘yan who has said the following. “I asked Imam abu Ja‘far (a.s.) this, ‘What are the rights of Allah on people?’” The Imam (a.s.) replied, “They must say what they know and abstain from saying anything that they have no knowledge of.’”
